top_signals.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 21
top_signals.summary: Oil steadied after a two-day gain as the US and Iran continued hostilities in the Middle East, while Yemen’s Houthi militants threatened to blockade Saudi Arabia’s maritime traffic in the Red Sea.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: The Fedorov Dismissal: On Trust, Technology, and Turnover
top_signals.summary:
Welcome to The Ukraine Compass, a weekly digest of Ukrainian commentary and analysis from across the political spectrum only for War on the Rocks members.Each Monday, we bring you a curated selection of articles from Ukrainian media offering insight into how Ukrainians themselves debate the issues shaping their country.Western coverage often narrows the view to the battlefield — these pieces widen it, revealing the texture of daily life, politics, and public argument in a nation at war. The perspectives gathered here are varied, candid, and often surprising, together forming a more complete picture of Ukraine as it really is.This week,

The post The Fedorov Dismissal: On Trust, Technology, and Turnover appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Russia's Financial Crisis Casts a Shadow Over Central Asia's Nuclear Future
top_signals.summary: Kazakhstan and Uzbekistan may soon have to rethink their plans to develop nuclear energy. Rosatom, the Russian nuclear entity that has agreements to build nuclear plants in both Central Asian nations, is experiencing major financial difficulties. Alexei Likhachev, Rosatom’s boss, has announced the entity is cutting its investment program by nearly 50 percent this year due to “poor economic conditions.” Likhachev also said Rosatom would strive to slash operating costs by 10 percent within the next 18 months. "We immediately…
